description: Two-signal proxy for AI adoption: (1) government procurement actions (contract awards and tender notices) and (2) public company annual filing mentions of AI deployment. Neither signal is a complete or symmetric measure. US data is substantially more transparent and automatable than China data.

government_procurement.description: Government AI contract awards and procurement notices — binding institutional decisions to acquire AI. Stronger adoption signal than mentions or expressed intent.

US.note: Federal contract awards (types A-D) whose descriptions match AI keywords (OR logic, deduplicated). Federal government only.

China.note: CCGP returned a response but the total count could not be parsed. China count unavailable for this snapshot.

company_filings.description: Public company annual filings mentioning AI deployment (exact phrase) via SEC EDGAR. US: 10-K. China proxy: 20-F (foreign private issuers, primarily Chinese ADRs).

US.note: Will be populated on next workflow run using upgraded fetch_adoption.py (v2).

China_proxy.note: 20-F = all foreign private issuers on US exchanges (primarily Chinese ADRs, not exclusively China). Will be populated on next workflow run.

top_examples.agency: Department of Health and Human Services

usaspending.name: USASpending.gov
usaspending.note: Official US federal spending data. No API key required.

ccgp.name: CCGP
ccgp.note: China central government procurement portal. HTML scraping, best-effort.

edgar.name: SEC EDGAR Full-Text Search (EFTS)
edgar.note: Public full-text search across SEC filings. No API key required.

methodology_note: Signal 1 (Government Procurement): binding decisions to acquire AI — stronger signal than mentions. US via USASpending.gov API; China via CCGP HTML scraping (often blocked from US IPs). Signal 2 (Company Filings): SEC EDGAR full-text search for 'AI deployment' in annual reports. 10-K = US domestic; 20-F = foreign issuers (primarily Chinese ADRs, not exclusively China). Updated daily.
caveats: 1. Scope asymmetry: US = federal + US-listed companies; China = central CCGP (often blocked) + ADR proxy (non-exclusive). 2. CCGP null = inaccessible, not zero. 3. 20-F overstates China. 4. Counts are orders/mentions, not deployment outcomes.
